name: Approach
intuition_and_algorithm: To print your name N times using recursion, the approach involves defining a recursive function that takes the current count as a parameter. The function checks if the count has reached N, if it has, the recursion terminates. Otherwise, it prints the name once and recursively calls itself, incrementing the count by one. This way, the name gets printed exactly N times as the recursion progresses. The key is to use the count to keep track of how many times the name has been printed so far and stop once it reaches N.

complexities.time: O(N) , we print our name exactly N times.
complexities.space: O(N) , stack space used for recursive calls.

code.python:
class Solution:
    # Recursive function to print name count times
    def printName(self, name, count, N):
        # Base case: if count equals N, stop recursion
        if count == N:
            return

        # Print the name
        print(name)

        # Recursive call with incremented count
        self.printName(name, count + 1, N)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    sol = Solution()
    N = 5
    name = "Ashish"

    sol.printName(name, 0, N)
